solutions.solution:
Sol. (i) (d) n = Side of bigger cube / Side of smaller cube = 15/3 = 5
∴ Required number of smaller cubes = 6(n - 2)² = 6(5 - 2)² = 6 x 9 = 54
(ii) (c) Required number of cubes = 12(5 - 2) = 12 x 3 = 36
(iii) (a) As cubes with three faces painted are always 8.
(iv) (a) As number of cubes with four or more faces painted is 0.
(v) (c) Required number of cubes = (n - 2)² = (5 - 2)² = (3)² = 27
(vi) (d) Total number of smaller cubes = (n)³ = (5)³ = 125
Sol. (c) A smaller cube with two sides painted is present on each of the 12 edges of the cube. Therefore, total number of cubes having paint on two sides = 12
